paper_title: Morphological and Textural Analysis of Mn/Co Binary Metal Catalysts Supported on Diatomaceous Earths

methods.description: This method involves the use of Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) and Energy Dispersive X-ray Spectroscopy (EDS) to analyze the surface morphology, metal particle size, and distribution of synthesized catalysts. It aims to provide an overview of the surface characteristics and confirm the presence of metal particles on the catalysts.

steps.description: SEM images of both raw supports and representative catalysts are illustrated in Figure 2.
steps.searchable_summary: capture SEM images of raw supports and catalysts

steps.description: Analyze SEM images to observe the diversity of sizes and morphologies of diatomaceous earths.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SEM images for size and morphology diversity

steps.description: Analyze SEM images at higher magnifications to observe metal particle size and distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze high magnification SEM images for metal particle size and distribution

steps.description: Use EDS analysis to confirm the presence of Mn and Co on all prepared catalysts.
steps.searchable_summary: perform EDS analysis to confirm Mn and Co presence

steps.description: Analyze specific surface area (SSA) and total pore volume of raw supports and catalysts.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SSA and pore volume of supports and catalysts

steps.description: Analyze adsorption isotherms to understand pore size distribution and metal particle influence.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze adsorption isotherms for pore size distribution
